proof_sketch: The mutation lies in an `else if (input.scalar_type() != torch::kBFloat16)` branch inside the `else` block of the outer `if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat32)` condition. The fixed input specification requires `dtype=torch.float32`, so the outer condition always takes the first branch (float32 kernel), and the mutated line is never executed. Consequently, the original and mutated kernel produce bitwise-identical results for all float32 inputs.
